title: conmon 100% CPU usage
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The conmon process is stuck at 100% CPU usage for unknown reasons. I work inside a toolbox (distrobox actually) container, and many times I find the conmon process that's stuck in a busy loop. I have only one podman container running on the system, and it keeps working even when conmon misbehaves, so I often don't even notice that something is wrong with podman.

When I notice I restart the container, which fixes it, and go about my day.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

No idea.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I have 3 conmon processes running at this moment, all seemingly monitoring the same container:

* conmon process started 2 days ago, 0 CPU usage
* conmon process started yesterday, 100% CPU usage
* conmon process started today, a couple hours after I resumed the system, 0 CPU usage

Might happen once a day after suspend/resume. 

Might be a regression, I have been running the same setup for almost a year, but I've started noticing this behaviour in the past month I reckon.

This container is started at boot with a systemd script generated my `podman generate systemd`.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 15:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: This was already fixed in conmon v2.1.3, https://github.com/containers/conmon/pull/343
